title: FIM Persistent-State Schema (fim-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the Financial-Integrity Monitor's `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to. The MVP shape is intentionally permissive on per-module structure (`additionalProperties: true` everywhere outside `_meta`); per-module strictness is the responsibility of the MBC sweeps that own each module's contract. The strict layer is the `_meta` block — `schema_version`, `last_updated`, `last_issue`, `monitor_slug`. The Applier engine bumps `_meta.last_updated` and `_meta.last_issue` on any cycle that lands at least one patch; null cycles are no-ops. Sprint-5 Phase 3 (BRIEF cf14eabd, E10 cure) adds a top-level `arrays` block declaring, for every persistent-state array FIM cares about, the merge `key_field` and a `key_constraint` (regex pattern OR string enum). FIM is currently paused (Lane B operator standing rule); BYTE_FLOOR_CANARY is NOT enabled here — that gating is in Phase 1's config flag. Schema authoring is approved for paused monitors.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. New canonical value: `aim-persistent-state-v1.0`. Legacy bake value `2.0` (string or number) is accepted during the AD-2026-04-26Y bake window.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change. Either ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD, what the Applier writes per Q5 ratification) or full ISO-8601 timestamp (legacy MBC-stamped). Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. The Applier increments this by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer; left untouched otherwise.

jurisdiction_risk_tracker.describe: Per-jurisdiction financial-integrity risk direction and enforcement/enablement balance (top-level array in persistent-state). `jurisdiction` is free-form (e.g. 'USA', 'EU', 'UK', 'UAE'). Free-form pattern — FIM jurisdictions are not a fixed closed set; ISO-3166-1 normalisation deferred per AGM precedent.

domain_tracker.developments.describe: Per-development entries nested under each domain_tracker item (D1-D5). `jurisdiction` identifies the regulatory or geographic context of the development. Free-form pattern — same jurisdiction vocabulary as jurisdiction_risk_tracker; not enum-locked.
